title: Implement evaluation value function and pairwise winner selection
description:
## Summary
Add a value function over evaluation records plus a pairwise winner-selection wrapper for comparing evaluation outcomes.

## Deliverables
- Implement a quantitative core `value(E)`.
- Implement a comparative wrapper `best_of(A, B)`.
- Permit `inconclusive` when runs are not comparable enough.
- Make AC1 primary with explicit guardrails for recall, precision, invalid RCA, sample-protocol mismatch, and related quality signals.

## Dependency note
This should follow the reference dataset assignment/build work when the value function is intended to judge deterministic regression runs.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation: quantitative value(E) and comparative best_of(A,B) with AC1-primary scoring, guardrails, protocol-comparability checks, and inconclusive outcome support.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented evaluation value function and pairwise winner selection.

Added module:
- plexus/cli/shared/evaluation_value_function.py

Capabilities:
- Quantitative core value(E) with AC1 primary score.
- Guardrail penalties for low recall / low precision.
- RCA validity penalty when root cause is required but missing.
- Comparative wrapper best_of(A,B).
- inconclusive outcomes for protocol mismatch, insufficient metrics, and ties.

Protocol comparability checks include mode/days/max_samples/sample_seed/score identity fields.

Tests added:
- plexus/cli/shared/evaluation_value_function_test.py
- pytest result: 4 passed.

This provides a reusable decision primitive for deterministic workflow acceptance and candidate ranking.
